text.content: We wake to the golden light pouring into our room. Beautiful rights peel down the point. Toni comes to collect money for our stay - how news has spread that we’re sleeping in the bungalow has spread; I don’t know. But we manage to get a 5L water out of it too.

rich_text.plain_text: We wake to the golden light pouring into our room. Beautiful rights peel down the point. Toni comes to collect money for our stay - how news has spread that we’re sleeping in the bungalow has spread; I don’t know. But we manage to get a 5L water out of it too.

text.content: Coffee on a grassy hill is obligatory. We watch the sun rise with our minds occupied by mindsurfing and watching the dolphins greet the day.

rich_text.plain_text: Coffee on a grassy hill is obligatory. We watch the sun rise with our minds occupied by mindsurfing and watching the dolphins greet the day.

text.content: Dogs are a huge theme in the Transkei. Damo is a border collie. Fred a Great Dane. And I a golden retriever. We start the run. Pull into a spaza shop for a lunch bar and coke. We take a few wrong turns. “Fastpacking - where the fuck are we?” Fred.

rich_text.plain_text: Dogs are a huge theme in the Transkei. Damo is a border collie. Fred a Great Dane. And I a golden retriever. We start the run. Pull into a spaza shop for a lunch bar and coke. We take a few wrong turns. “Fastpacking - where the fuck are we?” Fred.

text.content: We yo-yo around the three tourists who are hiking with a guide. After running away from them twice and both times losing the path, only for them to come past every hour or so.

rich_text.plain_text: We yo-yo around the three tourists who are hiking with a guide. After running away from them twice and both times losing the path, only for them to come past every hour or so.

text.content: After having to hike up a big hill through thick grass we get to the top and stop to call Mdumbi backpackers to check in and order three dinners. Damo goes for a swim which turns out to be a  huge mistake on the chafe department.

rich_text.plain_text: After having to hike up a big hill through thick grass we get to the top and stop to call Mdumbi backpackers to check in and order three dinners. Damo goes for a swim which turns out to be a  huge mistake on the chafe department.

text.content: Several kilometers later; the sun is baking and wind blowing us dry. We don’t have a huge amount of water but stop for a mama alles Central African peanut stew.

rich_text.plain_text: Several kilometers later; the sun is baking and wind blowing us dry. We don’t have a huge amount of water but stop for a mama alles Central African peanut stew.

text.content: My left ITB has been tickling a little bit. It’s definitely aggravated by the slanting beach. It takes more concentration to run now, but I am enjoying the slight challenge of staying mindful about my running form.

rich_text.plain_text: My left ITB has been tickling a little bit. It’s definitely aggravated by the slanting beach. It takes more concentration to run now, but I am enjoying the slight challenge of staying mindful about my running form.

text.content: We find a shady spot in the bushes with mottled sunshine. Eyes close slightly and we doze off. Someone comes walking past on his way to the Kraal backpackers - which is what we have done today!

rich_text.plain_text: We find a shady spot in the bushes with mottled sunshine. Eyes close slightly and we doze off. Someone comes walking past on his way to the Kraal backpackers - which is what we have done today!

text.content: After lunch Damo and I regain some energy. His chafe is hurting and he’s struggling along with me. Fred is thriving up ahead of with a spring in his step.

rich_text.plain_text: After lunch Damo and I regain some energy. His chafe is hurting and he’s struggling along with me. Fred is thriving up ahead of with a spring in his step.

text.content: The midday heat subsided and the energy from lunch arrives. We pass Lwandile with a golden light in the sky. The waves are messy but good. Damo and I share a smoke as we gear up for the last 7km.

rich_text.plain_text: The midday heat subsided and the energy from lunch arrives. We pass Lwandile with a golden light in the sky. The waves are messy but good. Damo and I share a smoke as we gear up for the last 7km.

text.content: My left ITB is sore on the steep descents. So I begin to just power hike the beautiful cow cut contours that take us to Mdumbi.

rich_text.plain_text: My left ITB is sore on the steep descents. So I begin to just power hike the beautiful cow cut contours that take us to Mdumbi.

text.content: The river crossing is pleasant and easy on the low tide. Siya is there to greet me when we walk into Mdumbi. A Swiss couple share the delicious rice, mince and veggies and we talk about her time as a doctor in Cape Town.

rich_text.plain_text: The river crossing is pleasant and easy on the low tide. Siya is there to greet me when we walk into Mdumbi. A Swiss couple share the delicious rice, mince and veggies and we talk about her time as a doctor in Cape Town.

text.content: Lisa is volunteering here along with Filen. Both of them offer interesting conversations in their own flavour. Filen talks about his time growing up on the Michaelhouse campus as a child of a teacher there. Lisa asks interesting questions and listens with presence. She had met my friend Niklas when he was here as well as knowing Gareth and Gemma.

rich_text.plain_text: Lisa is volunteering here along with Filen. Both of them offer interesting conversations in their own flavour. Filen talks about his time growing up on the Michaelhouse campus as a child of a teacher there. Lisa asks interesting questions and listens with presence. She had met my friend Niklas when he was here as well as knowing Gareth and Gemma.

text.content: Fred buys us two dumpies each. We receive six quarts. The beer calms my body and I relax. I’m in bed now. “It smells like deep heat and farts; reminds me of hockey tour” - Damo.

rich_text.plain_text: Fred buys us two dumpies each. We receive six quarts. The beer calms my body and I relax. I’m in bed now. “It smells like deep heat and farts; reminds me of hockey tour” - Damo.
